content:
Hi! I'm the CosmoLab AI assistant. I'm here to help you get started on your new product development project. This quick conversation will take about 10 minutes, and at the end, I'll prepare a structured project brief that our formulation and commercial team can review.

Let's start with the basics. Can you tell me the name of your brand and give me a one-line description of the product idea you have in mind?
My brand is called Soleil Studio. I want to develop a lightweight, daily SPF 50 moisturizer that doesn't leave a white cast — targeted at deeper skin tones.

Great concept — an SPF 50 daily moisturizer with no white cast is a strong market need, and deeper skin tones are historically underserved in this space.

A few important notes on this product type: SPF products are regulated as OTC drug products in the United States by the FDA, which means they require a Drug Facts panel, approved active sunscreen ingredients (typically zinc oxide, titanium dioxide, or chemical UV filters like avobenzone), and compliance with the FDA OTC monograph. If you're also planning to sell in Canada or the EU, there are different requirements in each market.

With that context — is the US your primary target market? And are you open to mineral-only actives, chemical UV filters, or a hybrid approach?
Yes, primarily US. I'm open to a hybrid approach — mineral and chemical filters — as long as the formula is elegant and doesn't pill under makeup.

Understood — hybrid UV filter system with a cosmetically elegant, non-pilling formula. That's very achievable.

Tell me about your target consumer a little more. You mentioned deeper skin tones — are you positioning this as a product specifically for melanin-rich skin, or as a broadly inclusive SPF that performs especially well on deeper complexions? And what's the price point you're targeting at retail?
It's specifically for melanin-rich skin — I want the brand to own that space. We're targeting $38–45 retail, clean beauty positioning, so no oxybenzone or octinoxate.

Clear positioning — a premium, melanin-rich skin SPF brand at $38–45 retail with a clean UV filter commitment. Excluding oxybenzone and octinoxate is very common for clean beauty claims and those two actives are already banned in several jurisdictions anyway.

Hybrid options that work well for minimal white cast include avobenzone + octisalate + a low-level zinc oxide at under 10% — this typically provides broad-spectrum coverage with a more elegant cosmetic profile on deeper skin tones.

A few packaging and volume questions: Do you have a container type in mind — pump bottle, dropper, or tube? And what launch volume are you thinking for your initial production run?
I'm thinking a 50mL pump bottle. For the first production run, probably 3,000 to 5,000 units — I'm launching DTC through my own website and potentially one boutique retailer.

Perfect — 50mL pump, DTC + boutique retail, 3,000–5,000 unit first run. That's a typical indie launch profile.

One last set of questions before I prepare your project brief:

1. Do you have a target launch timeline in mind (e.g., in 6 months, 9 months, 12 months)?
2. Have you worked with a contract manufacturer before, or is this your first formulation project?
3. Is there anything specific you want the formulation team to know — key ingredients you love, things you absolutely want to avoid, or a product you'd like to reference as a texture benchmark?

Target launch is 9 months from now. This is my first formulation project. For texture reference, I love the feel of Fenty Skin Hydra Vizor — that sort of gel-cream, lightweight hybrid. I want to avoid silicones and mineral oil. No synthetic fragrance.

Excellent — I have everything I need to prepare your project brief.

Quick summary of your project:
• Brand: Soleil Studio
• Product: Hybrid SPF 50 daily moisturizer for melanin-rich skin — no white cast
• Format: 50mL pump bottle, gel-cream texture (Fenty Skin Hydra Vizor-inspired)
• UV filter system: Hybrid (avobenzone + chemical filters + low-level zinc); no oxybenzone/octinoxate
• Exclusions: No silicones, mineral oil, or synthetic fragrance
• Regulatory: US OTC drug product (FDA monograph); clean beauty positioning
• Launch volume: 3,000–5,000 units (DTC + boutique retail)
• Timeline: 9 months
• Brand stage: First formulation project

Type 'submit' and I'll prepare your full Client Project Brief, which our team will review within 1–2 business days.
